title: Source Manifest Schema
description: Schema for source_manifest.json in curated real NBA snapshots. Defines source metadata, freshness governance, licensing, and validation tracking. Must not contain any execution, mutation, live-data, or logo-branding fields. M10-E2 patch: adds player_identities and roster_memberships as allowed future data_categories; adds per-file source_type/manual_review_required/live_eligible/data_freshness_warning/limitations fields; tightens forbidden categories and field names. M10-F6-D-prep patch: adds authorized_api_snapshot and authorized_provider_reference source_type values; adds optional provider lineage metadata fields (provider_name, endpoint docs/template, access/as_of/stale dates, license/key-redaction notes, secret-scan status, derived_from_raw_files) to per-file entries; per-file propertyNames forbid secret/key field names.

schema_version.description: Source manifest schema version, e.g. m10-b-v1

snapshot_type.description: Snapshot type classification. Must NOT be 'live' or 'realtime'.

as_of_date.description: Data cutoff date in YYYY-MM-DD format

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when snapshot was generated

source_name.description: Primary data source name, e.g. Spotrac 2025-26 contract/cap table

source_url.description: Source URL if publicly available, otherwise null

source_reference.description: Source reference (publication name, page title) when no direct URL is available

license_notes.description: License and usage terms for this snapshot

freshness_label.description: Human-readable freshness label, e.g. Pre-offseason freeze (as of 2026-06-25)

freshness_policy.description: Explanation of why this snapshot is not live/current and whether it satisfies the four live prerequisites (authorization, timestamp, auto-refresh, freshness policy)

live_eligible.description: Whether this snapshot is eligible to be called live/current. Must be false in M10.

stale_after_date.description: Date after which UI must show an additional stale warning; null if no explicit expiration

data_freshness_warning.description: Warning text directly consumable by UI, intelligence_summary, and agent_trace

limitations.description: List of data limitations and caveats

data_categories.description: Data categories included in this snapshot; may be empty for placeholder fixtures before data is loaded. Contracts/salaries/cap_sheets are FORBIDDEN in M10-E (deferred to M10-F or later). Injuries/scouting/rumors/live-status/current/latest are forbidden at any stage.

source_type.description: Provenance classification. 'llm_generated' and 'scraped_unreviewed' are forbidden and must not be added. 'authorized_api_snapshot' is for raw provider response files from an authorized API (e.g. Sportradar/SportsDataIO). 'authorized_provider_reference' is for normalized files derived from authorized raw snapshots.

provider_name.description: Provider identifier for authorized API sources, e.g. 'sportradar' or 'sportsdataio'. Optional; used with authorized_api_snapshot and authorized_provider_reference.

provider_endpoint_docs_url.description: Link to provider endpoint documentation (template, not key-bearing URL). Optional.

endpoint_template.description: Endpoint URL template with {placeholders}, NO API key. Example: 'https://api.sportradar.com/nba/trial/v8/en/teams/{team_id}/profile.json'. Optional.

access_date.description: Date the raw fetch was performed (ISO YYYY-MM-DD). Optional; used for raw snapshot entries.

stale_after_date.description: Date after which this per-file data must be considered stale; null if no explicit expiration. Optional.

key_redaction_notes.description: Notes describing how the API key was redacted from raw files and URLs (e.g. 'header auth used; query param key scrubbed'). Optional.

no_secret_in_raw.description: Confirmation that the raw file contains no API key, token, or secret. Optional; should be true for authorized raw snapshots.

secret_scan_status.description: Status of the secret scan on the raw file, e.g. 'passed', 'failed', 'skipped'. Optional.

derived_from_raw_files.description: Relative paths of raw files this normalized entry is derived from. Used by authorized_provider_reference entries to link back to raw snapshots. Optional.

manual_review_required.description: Per-file manual-review flag. All real player/roster data must set this to true.

live_eligible.description: Per-file live eligibility; must be false if present.

propertyNames.description: Forbidden per-file field names: secret/credential fields, forbidden data domains (salary/contract/cap/injury/rumor/scouting/live/depth), and execution verbs. additionalProperties:false already rejects unknown fields; this provides explicit enumerated protection.

per_file_sources.description: Per-file source tracking because teams/player_identities/roster_memberships/contracts/cap_sheets may come from different sources; may be empty for placeholder fixtures. M10-E2: extended with optional per-file source_type / manual_review_required / live_eligible / data_freshness_warning / limitations to support future player/roster lineage. M10-F6-D-prep: adds authorized_api_snapshot and authorized_provider_reference source_type values; adds optional provider lineage metadata (provider_name, endpoint docs/template, access/as_of/stale dates, license/key-redaction notes, no_secret_in_raw, secret_scan_status, derived_from_raw_files); per-file propertyNames forbid secret/credential field names.

allowed_usage.description: Allowed usage terms, e.g. non-commercial demonstration, internal review

redistribution_notes.description: Redistribution restrictions and notes, important for GitHub publication

file_hashes.description: SHA-256 hashes for each normalized file to detect tampering; may be empty for placeholder fixtures

replaces_snapshot_id.description: Previous snapshot_id this version replaces, or null if first version

reviewed_by.description: Reviewer identifier for auditability of validation_status

manual_review_required.description: Whether manual review is required; must be consistent with manifest.json

propertyNames.description: Forbidden field names: execution verbs, live/current/latest hints, logo asset references, salary/injury/scouting/rumor fields, and secret/credential fields (api_key, token, secret, password, etc.).
